etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 五(五) (wǔ) -> I
etymology.interpretation: The character 吾(wú, "I") combines 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") as the semantic component, signifying the speaker or the mouth of the speaker, with 五(五) (wǔ) as the phonetic component to provide the sound. The mouth represents speaking for oneself, linking to the pronoun 'I'.

examples.chinese: 吾爱此地，愿常驻于此(吾愛此地，願常駐於此)。
examples.pinyin: Wú ài cǐ dì, yuàn cháng zhù yú cǐ.
examples.english: I love this place and wish to stay here forever.

examples.chinese: 吾之思绪飘向远方(吾之思緒飄向遠方)。
examples.pinyin: Wú zhī sīxù piāo xiàng yuǎnfāng.
examples.english: My thoughts drift to distant places.

examples.chinese: 天地之间，吾心如水(天地之間，吾心如水)。
examples.pinyin: Tiāndì zhī jiān, wú xīn rú shuǐ.
examples.english: Between heaven and earth, my heart is as calm as water.
